key_insight: The paper replaces expensive eigenvalue-based adaptive coarse-space construction in overlapping Schwarz methods with transferable neural networks that predict both the needed coarse constraints and their basis functions.
engineering_relevance: This can substantially reduce online setup cost while preserving robustness for high-contrast PDE solvers in linear and nonlinear large-scale simulations.
method: Train a regression network to predict adaptive coarse basis functions.
Train a classification network to predict how many basis functions are needed for robustness.
Use sign-invariant losses and post-processing, then transfer the models across diffusion, elasticity, and p-Laplace problems without retraining.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports evaluation on scalar diffusion with high coefficient contrast and transfer tests to linear elasticity and nonlinear p-Laplace problems within a nonlinear Schwarz framework.
watch_item: Performance depends on reliable preprocessing/post-processing and may be sensitive to how well the learned predictions generalize across PDE families and coefficient contrasts.
paper_title: Learning Adaptive Coarse Spaces Using Transferable Neural Network Models for Linear and Nonlinear Overlapping Domain Decomposition Methods
